rows: Top 12 Most-Travelled Cities | Updated: April 2026 | Sources: Delhi Police, McAfee India Travel Research 2025, NCRB, Tourism Ministry data
1. The 'Master Scam List' tab contains every major scam mapped to all 12 cities with risk levels, financial impact, and prevention.
2. Each city has its own tab with the most prevalent scams in that location plus city-specific hotspots.
3. The 'Risk Heatmap' tab shows which scams are most active in which city at a glance.
4. The 'Emergency Contacts' tab has all helplines, tourist police numbers, and reporting channels — keep this on your phone.
5. The 'Pre-Travel Checklist' tab is a printable verification list before departure.
CRITICAL — Reported financial loss > ₹40,000 OR safety risk (drugging, assault). Immediate vigilance required.
HIGH — Common, organised, financial loss ₹5,000–₹40,000. Affects most tourists at least once.
MEDIUM — Petty overcharging, commission rackets. Annoying but small money loss.
• Delhi alone recorded 847 incidents involving foreign nationals (Delhi Police, 2025).
• 47% of Indian travellers fell victim to a travel scam in 2024–25 (McAfee India Research, Apr 2025).
• 53% of victims lost money; nearly 1 in 5 lost over ₹40,000.
• Fake hotel booking sites cost tourists an estimated ₹47 crore in 2025 (Cyber Crime units).
• Counterfeit travel agents near major monuments affect ~1,200 visitors monthly nationally.
Ranked by combined domestic + foreign visitor volume (Tourism Ministry & State Tourism Boards, 2024–25 data)
International gateway, business, Golden Triangle start
Beaches, nightlife, water sports, party tourism
Business, tech, garden city, gateway to South
Heritage, beaches, gateway to South, medical tourism
Backwaters, heritage, cruise, foreign tourists rising

Master Scam List.titleRow: Master Scam Catalogue — Top 12 Cities of India
Master Scam List.subtitleRow: Each scam includes: where it happens, who runs it, how it works, financial impact range, and verified prevention.
rows: Taxi/auto driver claims your booked hotel is closed, burned, overbooked, or in a 'riot zone'. Drives you to a partner hotel paying 30–50% commission. Currently most active at IGI Airport T3 (after midnight arrivals) and New Delhi Railway Station.
Call hotel directly before leaving terminal. Use only Uber/Ola or pre-paid taxi from the official airport counter. Never accept driver's verbal claim about your hotel — verify.

Fake offices with 'Govt. of India Tourism' signage sell inflated tour packages, fake train tickets, or non-existent hotel bookings. The real India Tourism office is at 88 Janpath Road, Delhi. Real IRCTC International Tourist Bureau is on the 1st floor of New Delhi Railway Station only.
Only book via incredibleindia.gov.in, irctc.co.in, or known platforms (MakeMyTrip, Booking.com). Never follow a 'helpful' stranger to an office. Verify location on Google Maps with reviews.

Lookalike URLs of Booking.com, MakeMyTrip, OYO; AI-generated logos and photos. Tourist pays advance via QR code, arrives to find hotel doesn't exist or has no booking. Cost tourists ₹47 crore in 2025 alone (Indian Cyber Crime data). Goa, Somnath, and Kashmir bookings most targeted.
Check URL letter-by-letter. Use only HTTPS, verified apps. Never pay via WhatsApp/UPI to unknown numbers. Cross-check hotel phone number on Google.

Friendly local introduces you to a 'family jeweller'. You're told you can 'export' gems duty-free and resell abroad for huge profit; they ship to your country. Stones are worthless glass/synthetic. Variant: a fake government 'official' calls later demanding fines.
NEVER buy gems/carpets to export. NEVER let a stranger guide you to a shop. Walk away the moment 'export profit' is mentioned. This is the same scam since the 1980s.

After paying at the official prepaid counter, driver claims he doesn't know your address, the road is closed, or there's a strike. Detours to commission shops or drops you at wrong hotel. Variant: a tout at the airport claims to be your booked Uber/Ola driver.
Match license plate against the app. Note driver name & phone. Share live location. Refuse all detours. At airports, walk to the official prepaid counter — don't engage anyone in the arrivals area.
All 12 cities (worst: Delhi, Kolkata, Chennai, Varanasi)
Driver says meter is 'broken', quotes flat fare 3–10x normal. In Mumbai it's rarer because of strict meter culture. In Bangalore mostly auto drivers at airport/railway.
Default to Uber/Ola/Rapido — they're cheap and GPS-tracked. If using auto, agree fare upfront after checking Uber price. In Mumbai insist on meter (it's the law).

Near railway stations, agents sell 'confirmed tickets' that are fake or for slow local trains sold as fast express. Variant: tout claims 'your train is cancelled' and herds you to a fake ticket counter to sell flights/tours at 5x rates.
Book ONLY via irctc.co.in, IRCTC Rail Connect app, MakeMyTrip, or RedBus. Verify train status on the official IRCTC app. Ignore anyone outside the station offering ticket help.
Agra (Taj Mahal), Delhi (Red Fort, Qutub Minar), Jaipur (Amer Fort)
Driver/tout near monument claims it's closed for a religious festival, lunch, VIP visit, or maintenance. Offers an 'alternative' site = commission shop / overpriced fort.
Walk to the actual ticket counter and confirm. Most ASI monuments are open Tue–Sun (Taj Mahal closed Fridays). Buy tickets only at official counters or asi.payumoney.com.
Varanasi (ghats), Pushkar, Delhi (temples), Jaipur, Agra, Udaipur

A 'pandit' ties a thread (kalava) on your wrist or applies tilak before you can refuse, then chants a 'blessing' and demands ₹500–₹10,000 'donation'. Most aggressive at Manikarnika and Dashashwamedh ghats in Varanasi.
Hands in pockets. Firm 'no, thank you' and walk away. Don't make eye contact. If thread is tied anyway, walk away — it costs nothing if you don't pay.
Agra (Taj Mahal), Delhi, Jaipur, Udaipur, Varanasi
Offers 'free' or cheap guided tour, then demands ₹2,000–₹5,000 at the end. May provide false historical 'facts'. Real ASI-licensed guides wear photo ID badges with QR codes.
Hire only licensed guides from the official ticket counter or via Incredible India app. Confirm price upfront in writing/screenshot.
Drugged Food / Drink on Trains & Buses
Delhi-Varanasi, Mumbai-Goa, all overnight long-distance routes
A friendly co-passenger offers tea, biscuits, samosa, or water. Sedative knocks you out for hours; bags, phone, cash, passport stolen. Reports increase Oct–March (peak tourist season).
Never accept ANY food/drink from strangers on trains or sleeper buses. Carry your own sealed water and snacks. Lock bags to the seat chain in sleeper class.
Refilled tap water in a sealed-looking bottle. Sometimes lookalike brands (Bisferi instead of Bisleri). Health risk = stomach infection, hospital bill ₹5,000–₹50,000.
Check the seal twists with a click. Inspect label spelling. Buy from sealed cases at chain stores (Reliance, More, 24Seven) when possible. Bisleri, Aquafina, Kinley, Himalayan are reliable brands.
Menus without prices, jet ski/parasail operators charging 5–10x and damaging equipment to demand 'damage' fees. Renting bikes/scooters with fake damage claims.
Confirm prices in writing/photo. Photograph rental bike from all angles before & after. Use only Goa Tourism-licensed operators (have govt. tag). Never hand over passport as deposit — only photocopy.
Mumbai (Colaba, CST), Delhi (Connaught Place), Bangalore (MG Road)
Stranger points out 'something' on your shirt; accomplice 'helps clean' while picking your pocket of phone/wallet.
Step back. Don't let anyone touch you. Walk to a clean spot and check yourself. Keep wallet in front pocket; phone in zipped bag.
All 12 cities — worst at standalone ATMs in tourist areas
Skimmer device on card slot + hidden camera or shoulder-surfer. Cards cloned, used internationally within hours.
Use ATMs INSIDE bank branches (HDFC, ICICI, SBI, Axis). Cover PIN. Wiggle the card slot before inserting. Set low daily withdrawal limits while travelling. Enable SMS alerts.
Shopkeeper/auto driver palms your ₹500/₹2000 note and returns a torn or fake one, claims you gave it. Common after a long bargaining session that distracts you.
Inspect every note received as change. State the denomination loud and clear when handing over. Use UPI (PhonePe, GPay, Paytm) — most vendors now accept; eliminates this scam entirely.

Tour operator on Instagram/WhatsApp offers cheap Kashmir/Goa/Manali package, demands UPI advance, blocks you. Or fake customer care numbers on Google → screen-share → drains account. Surge in 2025 with 'digital arrest' variant impersonating police/CBI.
Never pay UPI to unverified numbers. Never share OTP, screen, or bank app with anyone. Real police/CBI never call demanding payment. Verify operators on registered.tourism.gov.in.
Woman with baby asks not for money but 'milk powder' or 'baby food'. Leads you to a partnered shop where item is priced 5–10x. After you leave, the goods are returned to the shop and money split.
Don't engage. If you want to help, donate to a registered NGO (Goonj, Smile Foundation, CRY).
Delhi, Jaipur, Agra, Udaipur, Varanasi, Goa
Well-dressed young person strikes up a conversation, claims to be a student, offers to show you 'authentic local market' / 'cultural show' / 'family shop' = commission trap.
Be politely unavailable. 'No thanks, I'm meeting someone.' Walk away. Real students don't trawl tourist areas looking for foreigners.
Delhi (Paharganj, Main Bazaar), Mumbai (Colaba), Goa, Varanasi
Man flashes ID, claims he's checking for drugs/foreigners' permits. Demands to see passport, money, then 'fines' you on the spot.
Real Indian police do not collect on-the-spot fines from tourists. Demand to go to the nearest police station. Call 112 (national helpline) immediately. Never hand over passport — only show.
Goa (Anjuna, Vagator, Morjim), Manali (Old Manali, Kasol), Rishikesh

Local offers drugs at a beach/party; once accepted/handed over, accomplice 'cop' arrives, demands large bribe to avoid arrest. Punishment for narcotics in India is severe — even small amounts = years in jail.
Refuse all drug offers. NDPS Act in India is harsh; 1 year minimum for personal possession. Walk away from anyone offering 'something to relax'.
During festivals, vendors charge 5–10x for colours (gulal), flowers, garlands, sweets, ritual items. Sometimes they apply colour without consent then demand payment.
Ask price BEFORE handing over anything. Carry small change. Decline non-consensual colour application firmly.
Unauthorised forex changers offer slightly better rates than banks but short-count cash, give torn notes, or include fakes.
Use only RBI-licensed exchanges (look for FFMC license displayed) or bank ATMs. Count cash on the spot before leaving. Skip Paharganj/Colaba street changers.
Mandatory shoe removal at temple entry. Some 'caretakers' charge inflated 'safekeeping fees' (₹50–₹500 instead of ₹10) or your good shoes are swapped with cheap ones.
Wear cheap flip-flops / sliders to temples. Use the official locker (₹10–₹20) where available. Note your shoe spot.
Fake Cordelia / Costa websites take advance for cruises that never sail. Surged in 2025 per Goa MP's statement in Rajya Sabha.
Book only via cordeliacruises.com or authorised travel partners with verifiable office addresses. Never pay via random UPI.
Y = highly active in city | M = present but less common | (blank) = not commonly reported
Maximum vigilance city — 847 incidents involving foreigners in 2025 (Delhi Police).
IGI Airport Terminal 3 — taxi touts, fake driver scam (especially after 11 PM arrivals)
New Delhi Railway Station — fake tourist offices, train cancellation scam, hotel switch
Paharganj / Main Bazaar — fake police, forex cheat, drug setup, fake travel agencies
Connaught Place — fake government tourist offices, gem touts, pickpockets
Chandni Chowk / Old Delhi — pickpocketing, aggressive touts, rickshaw overcharge
Karol Bagh — fake travel agencies, fake hotel switch from airport drop
India Gate / Rajpath — photographers demanding inflated fees
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Driver claims hotel closed/full, takes you to commission hotel paying him 30–50%.
Sells fake train tickets, overpriced tours. Real one is at 88 Janpath Road.
New Delhi Railway Station 'tourist office' upstairs
Real IRCTC International Tourist Bureau is on 1st floor. Touts redirect you to fake one.
Plainclothes 'officers' demand passport check + on-spot fine. Real police don't fine on the spot.
Use Uber/Ola — they're 30–60% cheaper than tourist auto rates.
Yellow line during rush hour is hotspot. Front pocket only, zipped bag.
Bird poop / shirt stain distraction in CP
Two-person team. Step back, don't let anyone touch you.
USE: Delhi Metro (safest, cheapest, fastest for most routes)
USE: Uber, Ola, Rapido (always; they show fare upfront)
AVOID: Taking auto-rickshaws from outside hotels in tourist areas without app
AVOID: Anyone approaching you at IGI arrivals or NDLS station
Monument city, scams concentrated around Taj Mahal and Agra Fort.
Taj Mahal East/West Gate — fake guides, 'closed today' scam, ticket touts
Agra Cantt Railway Station — taxi overcharge, hotel switch
Sadar Bazaar — gem/marble inlay shop touts
Fatehpur Sikri — aggressive 'guides' and child beggars
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Touts near gates. Taj is closed only on FRIDAYS — verify yourself at the ticket counter.
Demand ₹2,000–₹5,000 after free 'tour'. Hire only ASI-licensed guides with photo ID at the counter.
Drivers commission you to shops claiming 'family workshop'. Same scam as Jaipur — overpriced fakes.
Auto/taxi inflated fares from Cantt station
Use prepaid taxi at station OR Uber/Ola. Quoted rates are 3–5x real.
Approach you with 'free photo' offer, then demand ₹500–₹2,000 per print.
Free shoe covers come with your Taj ticket. Don't pay anyone selling them.
USE: ASI website asi.payumoney.com for advance Taj tickets (skip queue)
USE: Licensed guide booth at the East/West gate
AVOID: Anyone offering 'special' Taj entry, sunrise tickets via WhatsApp
TIMING: Taj closed Fridays. Open sunrise to sunset.
Heritage and shopping capital — gem & textile scams are decades-old institutions here.
Johari Bazaar / Bapu Bazaar — gem touts, fake 'family jeweller' setup
Hawa Mahal & City Palace — commission tour drivers, fake guides
Amer Fort — 'closed today' touts, photographers, elephant ride markup
Jaipur Junction Station — taxi overcharge, hotel switch
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Friendly local → 'family jeweller' → 'export gems for profit' → glass beads. Lose lakhs.
Driver commissions you to specific shop. Prices 5–10x normal Bapu Bazaar rates.
Sets up the gem scam. Walk away from anyone unprompted.
Touts redirect you to a paid 'alternate viewing point' = commission shop.
Use Ola/Uber/Rapido. Auto from station to old city = ₹150 by app vs ₹500 quoted.
WhatsApp groups offering ₹500 'all monuments tour' drop you at commission shops.
Elephant ride at Amer overcharge & welfare issue
Govt rate is fixed (~₹1,100). Touts charge ₹3,000+. Also: ethical concerns documented by PETA India.
USE: Composite ticket (₹400) covers Amer, Hawa Mahal, Jantar Mantar, City Palace — buy at first site
AVOID: Any shop a driver/guide 'recommends' — they get 30–50% commission
BARGAIN: Start at 25–30% of quoted price in Bapu/Johari Bazaar
Most relaxed safety profile but highest concentration of cyber & tourism fraud.
Calangute & Baga beaches — water sports overcharge, beach shack inflated bills
Anjuna & Vagator — drug setup, party scams
Old Goa & Panaji — taxi mafia (Goa has notorious taxi unions blocking Uber/Ola)
Dabolim airport & Mopa airport — pre-paid taxi inflation
Online — fake hotel/villa bookings via Instagram & WhatsApp
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Goa MP raised this in Rajya Sabha 2025. Fake sites mimic real hotels, AI-generated photos. Lose ₹20K–₹2L.
Beach offer → 'cop' demands bribe. NDPS Act = 1+ year jail minimum. Refuse all offers.
Fake scratches added; deposit/passport held hostage. Photo entire bike before+after; never give passport.
No Uber/Ola in much of Goa due to taxi union resistance. Use GoaMiles app (govt-backed) or pre-fixed kadamba rates.
Different menu/pricing for foreigners. Always ask for printed menu with prices.
₹5,000 jet ski quoted as ₹2,000; demand 'damage' fees after. Use only Goa Tourism-licensed operators.
Fake websites take advance for non-existent sailings.
USE: GoaMiles app (official govt taxi app, fixed rates)
USE: Bookings via Booking.com, MakeMyTrip — verify hotel exists by direct call
AVOID: Buying drugs, ever (NDPS Act is harsh)
AVOID: Renting bike from random shacks — use Royal Brothers, Onn Bikes, Vroom apps
Lower scam intensity than Delhi/Agra — meter culture is strong; pickpocketing is the main risk.
CST / Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus — pickpocketing, fake guides
Colaba Causeway & Gateway of India — touts, fake police, gem scams
Juhu / Versova beach — water sport overcharge, food cart hygiene
Local trains — pickpocketing especially during rush hour
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Pickpocketing on local trains & at CST
Mumbai's #1 tourist crime. Front pocket, zipped bag, no phone in back pocket.
Demand 'foreigner check' + on-spot fine. Demand to go to police station instead.
Same export scam, slightly less aggressive than Jaipur.
Official Elephanta ferry is ₹220–270 from Gateway. Touts quote ₹500–800.
Photographer gives 'free' photo, then asks for ₹500.
Less common than Delhi but still happens at international arrivals after midnight.
USE: Mumbai local trains (cheap, fast) — but be alert in rush hour
USE: Uber/Ola available citywide; meters in regular cabs are reliable here
USE: Official Elephanta ticket counter at Gateway
Spiritual intensity = scam intensity. Highest concentration of religious-themed fraud.
Dashashwamedh Ghat & Manikarnika Ghat — fake priests, photo extortion
Vishwanath Gali — pickpocketing, shoe theft, aggressive shop touts
Varanasi Junction Station — taxi overcharge, hotel switch, fake guides
Boat operators along the Ganga — overcharge for sunrise/Aarti rides
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Ties thread before you can refuse, demands ₹500–₹10,000. Hands in pockets, firm 'no'.
Photo strictly forbidden anyway, but fake 'caretakers' threaten heavy fines if you have a camera. Don't take photos there at all — out of respect & safety.
Quoted ₹3,000–₹5,000; real rate is ₹500–₹800 for a shared boat, ₹1,500–₹2,500 private.
Fake guide claiming to be 'temple authority'
At Kashi Vishwanath, asks for 'special darshan fee' = pocket money.
Drugged tea / lassi from random shops
Bhang lassi shops sometimes much stronger than declared. Tourists hospitalised.
Cyclo/auto driver claims hotel closed → commission guesthouse.
Wear cheap sliders. Use the official locker.
USE: Pre-fix boat price BEFORE stepping in. Walk to next ghat if quoted high.
USE: Authentic guides via incredibleindia.gov.in or your hotel concierge
AVOID: Photography at Manikarnika & Harishchandra ghats (cremations)
AVOID: Bhang lassi unless you understand the dose
RESPECT: This is one of the holiest cities in Hinduism — modest dress essential
Tech & business hub — lower tourist scam volume; mostly transport overcharge & cyber.
Kempegowda International Airport — pre-paid taxi diversion, fake driver
Majestic Bus Stand & KSR Railway — auto refusing meter, fake ticket counters
Commercial Street & Brigade Road — pickpocketing, fake brand stores
MG Road & Church Street — bird-poop pickpocket scam reports
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Most rampant scam in Bangalore. ALWAYS use Rapido/Uber/Namma Yatri (official BMTC-backed app).
Fake driver at airport claiming to be your Uber/Ola
Verify license plate before getting in.
Fake brand stores in Commercial Street
Counterfeit Apple/Samsung accessories. Buy electronics at authorised stores only.
ATM skimming in standalone street ATMs
Fake job/visa scam targeting foreign workers
Recent rise — affects African and SE Asian tourists.
USE: Namma Yatri (Bangalore's official auto app, NO commission, fair fares)
USE: Bangalore Metro (Purple & Green lines cover most tourist spots)
AVOID: Cash transactions at street ATMs after dark
Lower scam intensity, but pickpocketing on Metro & at Howrah Station is significant.
Howrah Station & Sealdah Station — pickpocketing, fake porters, hotel switch
New Market / Hogg Market — aggressive touts, fake brand sales
Kalighat Temple — fake priests, donation extortion
Park Street — overcharge at bars/restaurants for foreigners
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Pickpocketing on Kolkata Metro & at Howrah
Front pocket, zipped bag. Especially on Howrah-Dum Dum line during rush.
Real porters wear red shirt + metal armband with number. Pre-fix price: ₹50–₹100/bag.
₹500–₹5,000 demanded. Same as Varanasi pattern. Walk away firmly.
Fake brand stores in New Market
Auto/taxi refusing meter or quoting flat
Use Uber/Ola. Yellow Ambassador taxis often refuse short distances.
Less common than Delhi but happens at Howrah.
USE: Yatri Sathi app (official Bengal govt taxi app)
AVOID: Withdrawing large cash on the street
Generally low scam intensity for tourists; transport & medical-tourism cyber fraud are main risks.
Chennai Central & Egmore Stations — auto overcharge, fake porter
T Nagar — pickpocketing in crowded markets
Marina Beach — hawker overcharge, photographer fees
Hospitals (Apollo, Fortis, MIOT) — medical tourism scams targeting foreign patients
Top Scams Active in This City
How It Works & How to Avoid
Chennai autos are notorious. ALWAYS use Uber/Ola/Rapido.
Fake 'patient coordinator' offers cheap surgery at top hospital, takes advance, disappears. Book directly with hospital international patient desk.
₹100 corn for tourists vs ₹30 for locals. Fix price first.
Especially Sowcarpet & Mint Street. Buy only from BIS-hallmarked retailers (Tanishq, GRT, Joyalukkas).
Less common than Delhi but happens.
USE: Apollo/Fortis international patient desk directly for medical tourism
AVOID: Booking medical procedures via WhatsApp 'consultants'
Lower scam intensity than Jaipur but same Rajasthan-style gem & textile traps near Lake Pichola.
Lake Pichola boating ghats — overcharge, fake 'palace tours'
City Palace area — gem touts, commission tours

Boat ride overcharge on Lake Pichola
Official rates posted at City Palace ticket counter. Ignore touts at the ghat.
Same as Jaipur. 'Family workshop' = commission. Walk away.
Several scam websites in 2025 — book via Booking.com/MMT only.
Use Uber/Ola — Udaipur has good coverage now.
USE: Official City Palace boat ticket counter for Lake Pichola
USE: Pre-paid taxi at Udaipur airport
BARGAIN: Hard at Hathi Pol — start at 30% of quoted
Adventure & honeymoon hub — drug setup and rental damage scams are the big ones.
Old Manali — drug offers, party scams, café overcharge

Top Scams Active in This City

Drug possession setup (Old Manali / Kasol nearby)
Charas offered → 'cop' demands bribe. NDPS Act jail risk. Refuse all offers.
Photo before+after, never leave passport, use known agencies.
Fixed by union; high but unavoidable. Hotel can sometimes negotiate.
Use only HP Tourism-licensed operators. Verify pilot license.
Fake Instagram/WhatsApp packages with advance UPI demand. Lose ₹20K–₹1L.
Photographer at Hidimba Temple / Mall Road

AVOID: Old Manali at night for solo travellers; drug offers are routine there

Lookalike Kerala Tourism websites. Pay advance, no boat. Book via keralatourism.org or trusted operator (Spice Coast, Lakes & Lagoons).
Fake antique / spice 'discount' shops in Jew Town

Operators ask ₹500–₹1,000 to 'pull the net'. Fixed local rate is ₹100–₹200.
Fake websites. Book only via cordeliacruises.com or authorised agents.
Some 'Ayurvedic clinics' have no licensed doctors. Check NABH-accreditation.
USE: Kerala Tourism's official site keralatourism.org for houseboat bookings
AVOID: Buying 'antique' spices in Jew Town without comparison

Ministry of Tourism. 24×7. Hindi, English + 6 regional languages.
Report online fraud (UPI, fake bookings, card cloning) within 24 hrs for highest recovery chance.
File complaint with screenshots & transaction IDs.
Dedicated team at Taj Mahal & Varanasi ghats.
